steps.q: Is there water in the fresh tank?
steps.no: Fill the fresh tank and retest.

steps.q: Does the pump run when a tap opens?
steps.no: Check the master 12 V supply, VB06 Pumpe 10 A fuse, pump voltage and earth.

steps.q: Is the frost-protection valve closed and is water staying in the system?
steps.no: Warm and reset the valve, then refill and purge.

steps.q: Is the pump filter clear and the suction hose airtight?
steps.yes: Inspect blocked outlets, kinked pipework or a failed pump.
steps.no: Clean the filter and repair air leaks on the suction side.

steps.q: Is any tap or shower valve partly open?
Is water discharging beneath or behind the motorhome?
steps.yes: Check the frost valve, tank drain and visible leaks.

steps.q: Can you hear or see a leak at the kitchen, bathroom or boiler connections?
steps.yes: Isolate and repair the leaking joint before continued use.

steps.q: Does the pump stop if its outlet is briefly isolated by a competent person?
steps.yes: A downstream leak or open valve remains likely.
steps.no: The pump pressure switch or pump assembly may be faulty.
Use the No water flow diagnostic first.

steps.q: Has air been purged from the hot taps until flow is steady?
steps.no: Open hot taps and fill the boiler fully.

steps.q: Is the frost valve closed and is the boiler retaining water?
steps.no: Warm, reset and refill the system.

steps.q: Does the heater have gas or mains energy and stable 12 V control power?
steps.yes: Check appliance controls, fault codes and heating time.
steps.no: Restore the missing energy source or 12 V control supply.

steps.q: Does the leak occur only when the pump is switched on?
steps.no: Check tank, filler, drains and gravity waste pipework.

steps.q: Does it occur with cold water only?
steps.yes: Inspect cold-side joints, pump outlet and tap connections.

steps.q: Does it occur when a hot tap is opened?
steps.yes: Inspect the boiler outlet, hot hose and mixer tap connection. The kitchen hot connection has known history.
steps.no: Dry all areas and test one outlet at a time to isolate the branch.

steps.yes: Warm the area according to the fitted valve instructions before resetting.

steps.q: Will the valve latch closed when the system is empty?
steps.no: Inspect the valve mechanism and fitted model instructions.

steps.q: Does it open again during filling?
steps.yes: Check temperature, valve installation and possible mechanical failure.
steps.no: Refill slowly and purge air from hot taps.
